q: A parallel plate capacitor has plates of area A separated by distance d between them. It is filled with a dielectric which has a dielectric constant that varies as Kx=K01+αx where x is the distance measured from one of the plates. If αd<<1, the total capacitance of the system is best given by the expression:
question: A parallel plate capacitor has plates of area A separated by distance d between them. It is filled with a dielectric which has a dielectric constant that varies as Kx=K01+αx where x is the distance measured from one of the plates. If αd<<1, the total capacitance of the system is best given by the expression:
solution:
Capacitance of element, dC=K01+αxε0Adx
∫1dC=∫0ddxK0ε0A1+αx
⇒1C=1K0ε0Aαln1+αd
Given αd≪1
⇒1C=1K0ε0Aααd-α2d22
⇒1C=dK0ε0A1-αd2
⇒C=K0ε0Ad1+αd2

subject: Physics
q: In the following circuit the switch Sis closed at t =0. The charge on the capacitor C1 as a function of time will be given by Ceq= C1C2C1+ C2
question: In the following circuit the switch Sis closed at t =0. The charge on the capacitor C1 as a function of time will be given by Ceq= C1C2C1+ C2
solution:
In given circuit capacitors C1 and C2 are connected in series , so equivalent capacitance will be 

1Ceq=1C1 +1C2 ⇒Ceq=C1C2C1+C2 

at t = 0, the switch is closed and capacitors start charged, at time t 

charge on the capacitor is given by 

q= q0 1-e-t/τ

where q0 is the maximum charge on the capacitor at steady state and τ is the time constant.

and q0 =CeqE and τ = RCeq

Then from the above equation

q= CeqE 1-e-t/RCeq

∴q= CeqE 1-exptRCeq
Both capacitor will have same charge as they are connected in series

question:

(Take ln2=0.69)
solution:
V=V01-e-tRC
50=1001-e-tRC
t=0.69×10-4 sec.

subject: Physics
q: A parallel plate capacitor with air between the plate has a capacitance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math>. The separation between the plate becomes twice and the space between them is filled with a medium of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math>. Then the capacitance becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is____.
question: A parallel plate capacitor with air between the plate has a capacitance of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math>. The separation between the plate becomes twice and the space between them is filled with a medium of dielectric constant <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn></math>. Then the capacitance becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi>pF</mi></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is____.
solution:
Capacitance of the parallel plate capacitor when air is present between the plates, C=ε0Ad=15 pF

Capacitance of the parallel plate capacitor when dielectric is filled in between the plates,

 C'=kε0Ad'=3.5ε0A2d=3.52×15 pF=1054 pF

Therefore, x=105.

subject: Physics
q: A capacitor is made of a flat plate of area A and a second plate having a stair-like structure as shown in figure. If the area of each stair is $\frac{A}{3}$ and the height is $d$, the capacitance of the arrangement is :
question: A capacitor is made of a flat plate of area A and a second plate having a stair-like structure as shown in figure. If the area of each stair is $\frac{A}{3}$ and the height is $d$, the capacitance of the arrangement is :
solution:
All capacitor are in parallel combination.
Also effective area is common area only
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow C_{\text {eq }}=C_1+C_2+C_3 \\ & \Rightarrow C_{\text {eq }}=\frac{A \varepsilon_0}{3 d}+\frac{A \varepsilon_0}{3(2 d)}+\frac{A \varepsilon_0}{3(3 d)} \\ & \Rightarrow C_{\text {eq }}=\frac{A \varepsilon_0}{3}\left(\frac{11}{6 d}\right) \\ & \Rightarrow C_{\text {eq }}=\frac{11 A \varepsilon_0}{18 d}\end{aligned}$

q: The work done in placing a charge of $8 \times 10^{-18}$ coulomb on a condenser of capacity 100 micro-farad is
question: The work done in placing a charge of $8 \times 10^{-18}$ coulomb on a condenser of capacity 100 micro-farad is
solution: Work done $=\frac{1}{2} \frac{\mathrm{q}^2}{\mathrm{c}}=\frac{\left(8 \times 10^{-18}\right)^2}{2 \times 100 \times 10^{-8}}=32 \times 10^{-32} \mathrm{~J}$

subject: Physics
q: A capacitor with capacitance <math><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> is charged to <math><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> . If the plates are pulled apart to reduce the capacitance to <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math>, how much work is done?
question: A capacitor with capacitance <math><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math> is charged to <math><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi></math> . If the plates are pulled apart to reduce the capacitance to <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math>, how much work is done?
solution:
Uinitial=Q22C1
Ufinal=Q22C2
Work done =Ufinal-Uinitial
=Q22C2-Q22C1
=5×10-622×2×10-6-5×10-622×5×10-6 
=254-52×10-6
=154×10-6 =3.75×10-6 J

subject: Physics
q: A parallel plate capacitor with plate separation 5 mm is charged by a battery. On introducing a mica sheet of 2 mm and maintaining the connections of the plates with the terminals of the battery, it is found that it draws $25 \%$ more charge from the battery. The dielectric constant of mica is $\_\_\_\_$.
question: A parallel plate capacitor with plate separation 5 mm is charged by a battery. On introducing a mica sheet of 2 mm and maintaining the connections of the plates with the terminals of the battery, it is found that it draws $25 \%$ more charge from the battery. The dielectric constant of mica is $\_\_\_\_$.
solution:
Initial capacitor has plate separation 5 mm with capacitance $C_1 = \epsilon_0 A / 5$.
After inserting 2 mm mica sheet, the gap remaining is 3 mm of air. The effective separation becomes: $d_{eff} = 3 + 2/k$ where $k$ is the dielectric constant of mica.
New capacitance: $C_2 = \frac{\epsilon_0 A}{3 + 2/k}$
Since battery remains connected, voltage is constant. The charge is: $Q = CV$
Ratio of charges: $\frac{Q_2}{Q_1} = \frac{C_2}{C_1} = \frac{\epsilon_0 A/(3 + 2/k)}{\epsilon_0 A/5} = \frac{5}{3 + 2/k}$
Given that charge increases by 25%: $Q_2 = 1.25 Q_1$
$\frac{5}{3 + 2/k} = 1.25$
$5 = 1.25(3 + 2/k)$
$5 = 3.75 + 2.5/k$
$1.25 = 2.5/k$
$k = 2$

q: Effective capacitance of parallel combination of two capacitors <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is <math><mn>10</mn><mi></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo>.</mo></math> When these capacitors are individually connected to a voltage source of <math><mn>1</mn><mi></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the energy stored in the capacitor <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is <math><mn>4</mn></math> times that of <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>.</mo></math> If these capacitors are connected in series, their effective capacitance will be:
question: Effective capacitance of parallel combination of two capacitors <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is <math><mn>10</mn><mi></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">μ</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi><mo>.</mo></math> When these capacitors are individually connected to a voltage source of <math><mn>1</mn><mi></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">V</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the energy stored in the capacitor <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> is <math><mn>4</mn></math> times that of <math><msub><mrow><mi>C</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>.</mo></math> If these capacitors are connected in series, their effective capacitance will be:
solution:
Given C1+C2=10μF    ...(i)
412C1V2=12C2V2
⇒  4C1=C2...(ii)
From equation (i) and (ii)
C1=2μF
C2=8μF
If they are in series
Ceq=C1C2C1+2=1.6μF

question: A bulb and a capacitor are connected in series across an ac supply. A dielectric is then placed between the plates of the capacitor. The glow of the bulb:

subject: Physics
q:
This question has Statement 1 and Statement 2 . Of the four choices given after the Statements, choose the one that best describes the two Statements.
Statement 1: It is not possible to make a sphere of capacity 1 farad using a conducting material. 
Statement 2: It is possible for earth as its radius is $6.4 \times 10^6 \mathrm{~m}$.
question:
This question has Statement 1 and Statement 2 . Of the four choices given after the Statements, choose the one that best describes the two Statements.
Statement 1: It is not possible to make a sphere of capacity 1 farad using a conducting material. 
Statement 2: It is possible for earth as its radius is $6.4 \times 10^6 \mathrm{~m}$.
options: Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is true, Statement 2 is the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is false, Statement 2 is true.
Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is true, Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is false.
solution:
Capacitance of sphere is given by:
$C=4 \pi \epsilon_0 r$
If, $C=1 \mathrm{~F}$ then radius of sphere needed:
$r=\frac{C}{4 \pi \epsilon_0}=\frac{1}{4 \pi \times 8.85 \times 10^{-12}}$
or, $r=\frac{10^{12}}{4 \pi \times 8.85}=9 \times 10^9 \mathrm{~m}$
$9 \times 10^9 \mathrm{~m}$ is very large, it is not possible to obtain such a large sphere. Infact earth has radius $6.4 \times 10^6 \mathrm{~m}$ only and capacitance of earth is $711 \mu \mathrm{F}$.

subject: Physics
q: A capacitor has air as dielectric medium and two conducting plates of area $12 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ and they are $0.6 \mathrm{~cm}$ apart. When a slab of dielectric having area $12 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ and $0.6 \mathrm{~cm}$ thickness is inserted between the plates, one of the conducting plates has to be moved by $0.2 \mathrm{~cm}$ to keep the capacitance same as in previous case. The dielectric constant of the slab is : (Given $\epsilon_0=8.834 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{~F} / \mathrm{m}$ )
question: A capacitor has air as dielectric medium and two conducting plates of area $12 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ and they are $0.6 \mathrm{~cm}$ apart. When a slab of dielectric having area $12 \mathrm{~cm}^2$ and $0.6 \mathrm{~cm}$ thickness is inserted between the plates, one of the conducting plates has to be moved by $0.2 \mathrm{~cm}$ to keep the capacitance same as in previous case. The dielectric constant of the slab is : (Given $\epsilon_0=8.834 \times 10^{-12} \mathrm{~F} / \mathrm{m}$ )
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{\mathrm{A} \varepsilon_{\mathrm{o}}}{\mathrm{d}}=\frac{\mathrm{A} \varepsilon_{\mathrm{o}}}{\left(0.2+\frac{\mathrm{d}}{\mathrm{k}}\right)} \\ & 0.6=0.2+\frac{0.6}{\mathrm{k}} \\ & \mathrm{k}=\frac{3}{2}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: Two metallic plates form a parallel plate capacitor. The distance between the plate is 'd'. A metal sheet of thickness d2 and of area equal to area of each plate is introduced between the plates. What will be the ratio of the new capacitance to the original capacitance of the capacitor?
question: Two metallic plates form a parallel plate capacitor. The distance between the plate is 'd'. A metal sheet of thickness d2 and of area equal to area of each plate is introduced between the plates. What will be the ratio of the new capacitance to the original capacitance of the capacitor?
solution:
Before the metal sheet is inserted, capacitance is given by C=ε0Ad.

After the sheet is inserted, the system is equivalent to two capacitors in series, each of capacitance C'=ε0Ad4=4C.
The equivalent capacity is now 1C'=14C+14C⇒C'=2C.

The ratio is CC'=12=1:2

q: If the charge on a capacitor is increased by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mi>C</mi></math>, the energy stored in it increases by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>44</mn><mo>%</mo></math>. The original charge on the capacitor is (in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math>)
solution:
Energy stored in a capacitor is given by E=Q22C.

Let initial charge be Q0.

Then, 1.44Q022C=Q0+222C

⇒1.2Q0=Q0+2

Q0=20.2=10 C

subject: Physics
q: Using a battery, a 100 pF capacitor is charged to 60 V and then the battery is removed. After that, a second uncharged capacitor is connected to the first capacitor in parallel. If the final voltage across the second capacitor is 20 V, its capacitance is : (in pF)
question: Using a battery, a 100 pF capacitor is charged to 60 V and then the battery is removed. After that, a second uncharged capacitor is connected to the first capacitor in parallel. If the final voltage across the second capacitor is 20 V, its capacitance is : (in pF)
solution:
New potential $=\frac{\mathrm{C}_0 \mathrm{~V}_0}{\mathrm{C}_0+\mathrm{C}}=\frac{\mathrm{V}_0}{3}$
$\begin{aligned} & 3 \mathrm{C}_0 \mathrm{~V}_0=\mathrm{C}_0 \mathrm{~V}_0+\mathrm{CV}_0 \\ & 2 \mathrm{C}_0 \mathrm{~V}_0=\mathrm{CV}_0 \\ & \mathrm{C} \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{C}_0\end{aligned}$

solution: As given in the figure, $6 \mu \mathrm{F}$ and $4 \mu \mathrm{F}$ are in parallel. Now using charge conservation Charge on $6 \mu F$ capacitor $=\frac{6}{6+4} \times 30=18 \mu C$ Since charge is asked on right plate therefore is $+18 \mu C$

Charges flow from $L$ to $R$ in both (a) and (b)
Charges flow from $R$ to $L$ in (a) and from $L$ to $R$ in (b)

solution:
Charge (or current) always flows from higher potential to lower potential.
Potential $=\frac{\text { Charge }}{\text { Capacitance }}$

q:
The displacement current of 4.425 μA is developed in the space between the plates of parallel plate capacitor when voltage is changing at a rate of 106 V s-1. The area of each plate of the capacitor is 40 cm2. The distance between each plate of the capacitor is x×10-3 m. The value of x is ,
(Permittivity of free space, ε0=8.85×10-12 C2 N-1 m-2) _______
question:
The displacement current of 4.425 μA is developed in the space between the plates of parallel plate capacitor when voltage is changing at a rate of 106 V s-1. The area of each plate of the capacitor is 40 cm2. The distance between each plate of the capacitor is x×10-3 m. The value of x is ,
(Permittivity of free space, ε0=8.85×10-12 C2 N-1 m-2) _______
solution:
Displacement current is given by id=ε0dϕEdt

Or id=ε0ddtEA, where E=qAε0

Or id=ε0ddtqAAε0=dqdt=ddtCV

Or id=CdVdt=ε0AddVdt

Putting the values, we have 

4.425×10-6=8.85×10-12×40×10-4×106 d

d=2×10-6×10-4×106×40

d=80×10-4=8×10-3 m

Hence, value of x=8.

subject: Physics
q: A parallel plate air capacitor has a capacitance $C$. When it is half filled as show in figure with a dielectric constant $K = 5$, the percentage increase in the capacitance is _______.
question: A parallel plate air capacitor has a capacitance $C$. When it is half filled as show in figure with a dielectric constant $K = 5$, the percentage increase in the capacitance is _______.
solution:
Initial capacitance of the air capacitor is given by:

$C = \dfrac{\epsilon_0 A}{d}$

When the capacitor is half-filled with a dielectric of thickness $d/2$, it can be considered as two capacitors connected in series.

Capacitance of the dielectric-filled part:

$C_1 = \dfrac{K \epsilon_0 A}{d/2} = \dfrac{2K \epsilon_0 A}{d} = 2KC$

Capacitance of the air-filled part:

$C_2 = \dfrac{\epsilon_0 A}{d/2} = \dfrac{2 \epsilon_0 A}{d} = 2C$

The equivalent capacitance $C_{eq}$ of the series combination is:

$C_{eq} = \dfrac{C_1 C_2}{C_1 + C_2} = \dfrac{(2KC)(2C)}{2KC + 2C} = \dfrac{2K}{K+1} C$

Substituting $K = 5$:

$C_{eq} = \dfrac{2(5)}{5+1} C = \dfrac{10}{6} C = \dfrac{5}{3} C$

The increase in capacitance is:

$\Delta C = C_{eq} - C = \dfrac{5}{3} C - C = \dfrac{2}{3} C$

The percentage increase in capacitance is:

$\% \text{ increase} = \dfrac{\Delta C}{C} \times 100 = \dfrac{2}{3} \times 100 \approx 66.67\%$

Answer: $66.67$

subject: Physics
q: The material filled between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor has resistivity 200 Ω m. The value of capacitance of the capacitor is 2 pF. If a potential difference of 40 V is applied across the plates of the capacitor, then the value of leakage current flowing out of the capacitor is: (given the value of relative permittivity of material is 50)
question: The material filled between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor has resistivity 200 Ω m. The value of capacitance of the capacitor is 2 pF. If a potential difference of 40 V is applied across the plates of the capacitor, then the value of leakage current flowing out of the capacitor is: (given the value of relative permittivity of material is 50)
solution:
ρ=200 Ωm

K=50

C=ϵ0KAd=2×10−12 F

R=ρdA

I=VR=AVρd=Vρ⋅Cϵ0K=40200×2×10−128.85×10−12×50=0.9mA

q: Calculate the amount of charge on capacitor of 4 μF. The internal resistance of battery is 1Ω:
question: Calculate the amount of charge on capacitor of 4 μF. The internal resistance of battery is 1Ω:
solution:
Using i=54+1=1 A

∴VAB=i×4=4 V ∴VAC=VCB=2 Volt

∴q on 4μF=CVAC=8μC

subject: Physics
q: The distance between two plates of a capacitor is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math> and its capacitance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>, when air is the medium between the plates. If a metal sheet of thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>d</mi></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> and of the same area as plate is introduced between the plates, the capacitance of the capacitor becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> . The ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfrac></math> is
question: The distance between two plates of a capacitor is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>d</mi></math> and its capacitance is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math>, when air is the medium between the plates. If a metal sheet of thickness <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>d</mi></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></math> and of the same area as plate is introduced between the plates, the capacitance of the capacitor becomes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> . The ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>C</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mfrac></math> is
solution:
The formula of capacitance is C1=ε0Ad

The dielectric constant of metal sheet is Kmetal=∞

The new value of capacitance is 

C2=ε0Ad-t+tKmetal=ε0Ad-2d3+0=3C1

Clearly, the ratio,

C2C1=3C1C1=3:1

q:
In a parallel plate capacitor set up, the plate area of capacitor is 2 m2 and the plates are separated by 1 m. If the space between the plates are filled with a dielectric material of thickness 0.5 m and are 2 m2 (see figure) the capacitance of the set-up will be ε0 ________.

(Dielectric constant of the material =3.2)

question:
In a parallel plate capacitor set up, the plate area of capacitor is 2 m2 and the plates are separated by 1 m. If the space between the plates are filled with a dielectric material of thickness 0.5 m and are 2 m2 (see figure) the capacitance of the set-up will be ε0 ________.

(Dielectric constant of the material =3.2)

(Round off to the Nearest Integer)
solution:
The given system can be considered as a series combination of two capacitors. The equivalent capacitance can be given as,

1C=1C1+1C2⇒1C=d2Kε0A+d2ε0A

⇒C=ε0Ad2K+d2=2ε0AdK+d

=2×2ε013.2+1=4×3.24.2ε0

=3.04ε0
